hero.headline: LPU Online MBA in Hospital and Healthcare Management: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: Lovely Professional University's UGC-DEB approved online MBA with a Hospital and Healthcare Management specialisation. NIRF #31 University, NAAC A+. Rs 2,00,000 standard fee, Rs 1,61,600 with 20% Student Grant. Zero-cost 24-month EMI at Rs 6,733/month. Hospital Administration, Healthcare Operations, Health Informatics, Healthcare Finance, and Quality Accreditation in Sem 3-4.

sections.tldr: LPU Online MBA Hospital and Healthcare Management: NIRF #31, NAAC A+, UGC-DEB approved, Rs 2L standard or Rs 1.61L with 20% grant, zero-cost 24-month EMI. Healthcare management depth with Hospital Administration, Healthcare Operations, Health Informatics, Healthcare Finance Management, Quality and Accreditation, and Community Health. Strong for hospital administration, healthcare operations, and health management careers.

about.body:
The LPU Online MBA in Hospital and Healthcare Management is offered by Lovely Professional University's online division. LPU holds NAAC A+ accreditation, NIRF #31 University ranking (2025), and NIRF #44 in Management. The online programme is UGC-DEB approved.

The Hospital and Healthcare Management specialisation delivers dedicated Sem 3-4 subjects covering Hospital Administration and Planning, Healthcare Operations Management, Health Informatics, Healthcare Finance Management, Quality and Accreditation in Healthcare, and Community Health Management. These subjects are designed for hospital administrators, healthcare operations managers, and health professionals targeting management roles.

LPU's large medical and paramedical student and alumni community gives its Healthcare Management MBA a distinctive healthcare network. Punjab and North India have a growing private hospital sector with large hospital chains in Amritsar, Ludhiana, Jalandhar, and Chandigarh. The zero-cost EMI makes this the most affordable NIRF-ranked healthcare management MBA for working healthcare professionals.

sem3Subjects.name: Hospital Administration and Planning
sem3Subjects.description: Design hospital facility plans, manage bed capacity, organise department adjacency, and lead hospital expansion projects.

sem3Subjects.name: Healthcare Operations Management
sem3Subjects.description: Manage OT scheduling, ICU capacity utilisation, clinical pathway design, and hospital operations performance management.

sem3Subjects.name: Health Informatics
sem3Subjects.description: Implement hospital information systems, manage EMR and EHR integration, and apply health data standards for hospital IT management.

sem3Subjects.name: Corporate Strategy and Entrepreneurship
sem3Subjects.description: Develop hospital group strategy, specialty expansion planning, and healthcare entrepreneurship for clinic and diagnostic network ventures.

sem3Subjects.name: Generic Elective I
sem3Subjects.description: Elective subject from the GE basket chosen by the student to complement the Healthcare Management specialisation.

sem3Subjects.name: Seminar on Summer Training
sem3Subjects.description: Present healthcare management summer project findings to faculty panel, developing hospital administration communication skills.

sem4Subjects.name: Capstone Project
sem4Subjects.description: Complete an applied healthcare management project on hospital quality improvement, operations efficiency, or healthcare finance strategy.

sem4Subjects.name: Healthcare Finance Management
sem4Subjects.description: Manage hospital revenue cycle, apply healthcare budgeting, negotiate insurance tariffs, and manage PMJAY empanelment finance.

sem4Subjects.name: Quality and Accreditation in Healthcare
sem4Subjects.description: Prepare for NABH, JCI, and NABL accreditation, implement clinical quality indicators, and manage healthcare audit processes.

sem4Subjects.name: Community Health Management
sem4Subjects.description: Design community health outreach programmes, manage primary health centre operations, and apply public health management principles.

sem4Subjects.name: Generic Elective II
sem4Subjects.description: Elective subject from the GE basket chosen by the student to complement the Healthcare Management specialisation.

syllabus.note: Sem 1-2 shared core subjects across all LPU Online MBA specialisations. See the main LPU Online MBA page for core subject list.

whoHires.body:
Hospital and Healthcare Management MBA graduates from LPU Online are hired across these industries and roles:

**Industries:** Private hospital chains, Diagnostic chains, Pharmaceutical companies, Healthcare consulting, Health insurance, Public health organisations

**Entry-level roles:** Hospital Administrator, Healthcare Operations Manager, Quality Manager (NABH), Revenue Cycle Analyst, Health Informatics Executive

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** Hospital Manager, Head of Operations (hospital), Healthcare Project Manager, COO (clinic network)

**Senior roles (7+ years):** Hospital CEO, VP Healthcare Operations, Head of Medical Affairs, Healthcare Group Director

**Top hiring organisations:** Fortis Healthcare, Max Healthcare, Apollo Hospitals, Narayana Health, Manipal Hospitals, Medanta, Religare Health Insurance, Star Health Insurance, Niva Bupa, NABH-accredited hospital chains, Punjab state government hospitals, Chandigarh PGI network hospitals

skills.body:
The LPU Hospital and Healthcare Management specialisation builds skills that hospital administration and healthcare management employers test at COO-track levels:

**Technical skills:**
- Hospital Administration and Planning: hospital facility planning, bed capacity management, department planning, and hospital expansion management for hospital administrator roles
- Quality and Accreditation in Healthcare: NABH and JCI accreditation preparation, clinical quality indicator management, and healthcare audit process management for quality manager roles
- Health Informatics: EMR and EHR system implementation, hospital information system management, and health data standard application for health IT management roles
- Healthcare Finance Management: revenue cycle management, hospital budgeting, insurance tariff negotiation, and PMJAY empanelment finance for healthcare finance roles

**Applied skills:**
- Healthcare Operations Management: OT scheduling, ICU capacity management, and clinical pathway design for hospital operations management
- Community Health Management: primary health centre management, community outreach programme design, and public health management

**Soft skills:** Managing doctor-administrator relationships, presenting hospital performance metrics to boards, negotiating insurance reimbursement rates, and leading hospital accreditation preparation teams.

comparisons.body:
Comparing LPU Online MBA Hospital and Healthcare Management against peers:

**vs Amity Online MBA Hospital Healthcare Management:** Amity is NAAC A+ and NIRF #22 at Rs 2,25,000. Amity Healthcare is a strong all-India brand with comprehensive hospital management curriculum. LPU Healthcare Management at Rs 1,61,600 with grant is significantly more affordable. LPU offers zero-cost EMI; Amity does not. For North India hospital management careers on a budget, LPU is a clear value proposition.

**vs SSODL Online MBA Hospital and Health Care Management:** SSODL (Symbiosis) is NAAC A++ and NIRF #24 at Rs 3,15,000-3,70,000 with interest-bearing EMI. SSODL adds dedicated Super Speciality Hospital Administration and dedicated MIS for Hospitals subjects. For Pune-region hospital chains, SSODL carries stronger brand recognition. For North India hospital management careers at significantly lower fees with zero-cost EMI, LPU is the accessible alternative.

**vs MUJ Online MBA Healthcare (if offered):** MUJ offers healthcare management content within broader programme tracks. LPU Hospital and Healthcare Management is a dedicated full specialisation with distinct Hospital Administration, Health Informatics, Healthcare Finance, Quality Accreditation, and Community Health subjects. For dedicated healthcare management credentials at this price tier, LPU offers the more structured specialisation.

fees.body:
The LPU Online MBA in Hospital and Healthcare Management offers 2 fee tracks:

**Track 1 - Standard Fee**:
- Registration fee: Rs 1,000 (one-time)
- Total program fee: Rs 2,00,000
- Semester fee: Rs 50,000 per semester across 4 semesters

**Track 2 - With 20% Student Grant**:
- Registration fee: Rs 1,000 (one-time)
- Total program fee: Rs 1,61,600 after 20% Student Grant
- Semester fee: Rs 40,400 per semester

**Zero-Cost EMI option (Track 2)**:
- 24-month zero-cost EMI: Rs 6,733 per month
- No interest charged. No processing fee from the university.
- EMI is routed through the university's marketing partner.

The 20% Student Grant is applied at admission. Confirm current grant eligibility and applicable conditions with our counsellor before applying.

EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with LPU each quarter. Please reconfirm current fees and grant eligibility with our counsellor before any payment. Fees listed are indicative.

certificate.body: The degree certificate is issued by Lovely Professional University (LPU) and carries the LPU name with the MBA designation. The Hospital and Healthcare Management specialisation is noted on the marksheet and transcript. Online mode is identified per UGC-DEB 2020 regulations. The certificate is valid for corporate hiring, PSU eligibility, and higher education admission across India.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from LPU Online MBA Hospital and Healthcare Management alumni. Ratings from post-completion surveys.

items.name: Dr. Paramjit Kaur
items.liked: Quality and Accreditation and Hospital Administration subjects were directly applicable to my hospital management role. Zero-cost EMI made it very accessible for a working hospital administrator.
items.disliked: Healthcare Finance Management could include more case studies on TPA negotiation and PMJAY billing.
items.body: I had 5 years as a hospital administrator and the LPU Healthcare MBA helped me move to hospital manager level. The Rs 1.61L with zero-cost EMI was the key factor. LPU brand is recognised at North India private hospital groups.

items.name: Gaurav Nanda
items.liked: Health Informatics and Healthcare Operations Management subjects were excellent and practically applicable.
items.disliked: Community Health Management was less relevant for private hospital careers but important for public health roles.
items.body: Good programme for healthcare management career advancement. Chandigarh hospital groups recognised the LPU credential. The zero-cost EMI was the deciding factor over more expensive alternatives.

items.name: Swati Sharma
items.liked: NABH accreditation coverage in Quality subject was very practical. Hospital Administration planning concepts were directly applicable.
items.disliked: Placement support for healthcare-specific roles was limited. Self-sourcing was necessary.
items.body: Strong healthcare management content at an affordable price. For Punjab and North India hospital careers, LPU is a recognised and cost-effective choice. The zero-cost EMI made it an easy decision.

faqs.question: How does the LPU 20% Student Grant work?
faqs.answer: The 20% Student Grant reduces the standard Rs 2,00,000 fee to Rs 1,61,600. It is applied at admission. Confirm current grant eligibility conditions with our counsellor before applying, as grant availability may vary by intake cycle.

faqs.question: Is LPU Online MBA zero-cost EMI genuinely interest-free?
faqs.answer: Yes. The 24-month zero-cost EMI at Rs 6,733 per month carries no interest charge and no processing fee from the university. It is routed through the university's marketing partner. Confirm current EMI terms before committing.

faqs.question: Does LPU Healthcare MBA cover NABH accreditation?
faqs.answer: Yes. Quality and Accreditation in Healthcare is a dedicated Sem 4 subject covering NABH and JCI accreditation preparation, clinical quality indicator management, and healthcare audit processes. This is directly applicable to hospital quality manager and accreditation coordinator roles.

faqs.question: Is LPU Healthcare MBA suitable for doctors moving into management?
faqs.answer: Yes. The programme covers hospital strategy, operations, finance, and quality from a management perspective rather than a clinical one. Doctors moving into medical director, COO, or hospital CEO roles can benefit from the management and administrative frameworks the programme provides.

faqs.question: Is LPU Online MBA NIRF ranked?
faqs.answer: Yes. Lovely Professional University is ranked NIRF #31 (University category, 2025) and NIRF #44 in the Management category. This places LPU among the top-ranked universities offering UGC-DEB approved online MBAs in India.

chooseIf: You are a hospital administrator, healthcare operations manager, or health professional targeting management roles in North India hospital chains.
You want a UGC-DEB approved MBA from a NIRF #31 university at Rs 1,61,600 (with grant) or Rs 2,00,000 standard.
You want 24-month zero-cost EMI and dedicated NABH Quality Accreditation and Healthcare Finance coverage.
consider: If Super Speciality Hospital Administration and deeper hospital management depth is required, SSODL Online MBA Hospital and Healthcare Management offers more specialised coverage at Rs 3.15L.
If pan-India hospital management brand recognition is the priority, Amity Online MBA Hospital Healthcare Management at Rs 2.25L is well recognised.
If the target is large Pune or western India hospital chains, SSODL carries stronger regional brand recognition.
